label: Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED, FRONT-BACK split, GR style — the entire front (street-facing) half is public: great_room + kitchen, open-plan (gr style, no separate dining room). The entire rear half is private, arranged as ONE row: master, ensuite, common bath, and standard bedroom side by side (in that order), each dooring directly across the public/private boundary wall except ensuite (master's exclusive, no public door) and common (which shares its wet wall with ensuite instead). No hallway.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's EAST wall. Ensuite and common cluster together in the middle of the row, between master and standard.
ensuite and common share a plumbing wall — the two-bath cluster in the middle of the rear row, between master and standard.

common bath's east wall = standard's west wall — shared wall only, no door (bedroom<->common-bath is always wall-only per the codebase's generic rule). Acoustic buffer between the bath cluster and standard.
master's south wall = great_room's north wall at the front/rear boundary — a real door, no hall. Master reaches the rest of the house only through this one door.
standard's south wall overlaps great_room's north wall — direct door. 1.2 m floor gives 0.8 m clear door + 0.15 m frame each side.

common bath's only door — south wall opens into great_room. With ensuite (wet_core, no door) on one side and standard (wall-only) on the other, this is common's sole access, same idiom as the squarish side_split_bath_gr family: both bedrooms reach the bath by stepping out through their own door into the public band and back in.

great_room + kitchen form one open LDK (gr style) — consistent with every other wide-shell topology in the catalog. The solver's global LDK rule forces kitchen+great_room to stack front-to-rear (never side-by-side); the front_to_rear_stacks hint below pins great_room in front, kitchen behind.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Splitting the lot front-back halves the usable depth per band compared to the vertical-split topologies, so compact lots get tight fast — the whole private band has to fit master, ensuite, common, and standard side by side in one row of depth. auto_apply floors kitchen width and both bath areas so neither collapses to its hard minimum by default; master is capped at 18 sqm so its priority weight doesn't crowd the bath cluster or standard sharing its row. preferred_apply bumps ensuite and kitchen further where the envelope allows.

setback_elements.note: placed on the GREAT ROOM side (left), not the kitchen side — deviates from the catalog default of pairing carport with kitchen's side, because the dirty kitchen already claims the kitchen-side setback here (kitchen is at the front, not the rear, so it can't push its service tail into a rear setback like every other topology does).

moved from rear_setback to side_setback (kitchen-side/right) — a direct consequence of the front-back flip. Kitchen sits in the front band near the street in this topology, so it has no rear wall to extend a service tail from; the dirty kitchen instead extends off kitchen's outer side wall.
grouped with dirty_kitchen on the kitchen side for the same reason.
on the great room side, opposite the kitchen/service side.

notes: Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED, FRONT-BACK split, GR style. Every other topology in the catalog splits the lot vertically (bedrooms on one side, LDK on the other, both running front-to-rear). This one splits it horizontally instead: the entire front half of the house is public, the entire rear half is private, and one wall spanning the full width separates them.

Revision history: v1 (2026-07-13) used a plain living_room instead of great_room and was squarish-only (misfiled under wide/) — infeasible everywhere due to a mathematical contradiction between zone_split and the solver's hardcoded kitchen-rear-wall pin. v2 (2026-07-14) switched to great_room + kitchen (gr style) and corrected target_shell to wide. v3 (2026-07-14, WORKING): three solver-level fixes made this topology solve for real, verified end-to-end (14x11, COMPLIANT, score 23.50, 0 warnings, no fallback): (1) the kitchen-rear pin is now skipped when zone_split explicitly declares kitchen as a front/public room; (2) a new topology.ldk_horizontal flag (set true below) disables the solver's hardcoded LDK vertical-stacking rules, which otherwise force kitchen+great_room to stack front-to-rear and directly contradict a horizontal (side-by-side) LDK; (3) great_room was removed from left_anchored (only master stays) — forcing BOTH master and great_room onto the exact same left edge locked great_room into a rigid stacked relationship with master that made it geometrically impossible to also reach standard's adjacency on the far right, regardless of lot size (confirmed infeasible up to 34x34 m before this fix). Full diagnostic trail in project memory: front-back-split-topology-solver-bug.
Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='left'), matches the actual solved geometry:
Rear (private, one row): [master | ensuite | common | standard]
One wall spans the FULL WIDTH, dividing the two bands.

No hallway. Master and standard each door directly across the front/rear boundary wall into great_room. Ensuite is master's exclusive (wet_core with common, door only from master). Common's only door faces great_room — same circulation idiom as 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr's non-hall siblings: both bedrooms reach the bath by stepping into the shared public room and back in.
Wet stack: ensuite and common share a plumbing wall (wet_core) in the middle of the row.

IMPORTANT deviation from the rest of the catalog: because kitchen sits at the FRONT of the house (street side) rather than the rear, the dirty kitchen and service area can't extend into a rear_setback the way they do in every vertical-split topology — there's no rear wall behind kitchen to extend from. They're relocated to the side_setback on kitchen's side instead. Carport is pushed to the opposite side (great room's side) to avoid crowding that same setback strip.
Floor band: sized against a 14x11 m lot (envelope 10x7 = 70 sqm, ratio 1.43, genuinely wide).
When brief.carport_side='right', the runner mirrors the topology: great_room/carport move right, kitchen/service move left.
Fallback: 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr — drops to the standard vertical-split single-bath hall topology on lots too shallow to support two full-width bands.
